name: docs-migration-sprint
summary: 3 Sonnet writers + 1 Opus editor. Bulk migration of docs (framework version bumps, brand renames, terminology changes).
when_to_use: Large doc-only migrations with clear find-replace pattern but requiring context-aware rewriting (not a pure sed pass).
file_ownership: Sharded by alphabet to avoid collisions. No shared files. Each writer works in an isolated worktree.
coordination: 1. Editor surveys all docs, produces a migration spec: what's changing, examples of before/after, style guide.
2. 3 writers run in parallel; each rewrites their shard against the spec.
3. Editor reviews the merged result: consistency, tone, cross-references. Fixes any drift.
quality_gates: Post-writer: link-checker passes on all docs.
Post-editor: grep for old terms returns 0 matches (or expected exceptions list only).
cost_estimate: ~$1-3 for a 50-file migration (3 Sonnet + 1 Opus editor).
anti_patterns: Running writers without the spec — outputs diverge stylistically.
Editor rewrites writer outputs instead of nudging — defeats the parallelism.
